archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: CI pipeline entry point — orchestrates analysis and validation steps, invoked as a CLI command
Service module that configures MCP servers, skills, and permissions during init — business logic orchestration, not a CLI command itself
Shared help navigation, typo correction, and search utilities used across the CLI — no I/O side effects, high fan-in utility
Pure-function analysis engine for adaptive workflow adjustment — business logic, no I/O
Review gate service — prompts for approval and coordinates completion validation
Pure functions for detecting agent spin (no tool calls) — stateless, no I/O, reusable logic
Pure functions for stuck-task detection — stateless, no I/O, mirrors spin.ts pattern
Pure functions that produce run summary data from tool call/command/test records
Workflow analysis engine — identifies bottlenecks and failure patterns from historical run data
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for completion validation functions
CLI provider agent loop — orchestrates task execution via CLI, core runtime service
Periodic heartbeat writer — infrastructure utility that updates lastActivityAt timestamps
API provider agent loop — main Anthropic SDK tool-use loop, core runtime service
Shared lifecycle operations used by both API and CLI loops — extracted common logic, high fan-in
Displays task info to terminal before agent work begins — pure presentation utility
Token budget check logic — pure functions, types for budget enforcement
Re-exports token usage parsing functions from llm-client with backward-compatible aliases
Brief assembly — builds TaskBrief from PRD data, orchestrating multiple data sources
Builds the system prompt string for the agent — pure function, no side effects
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for tool definitions and dispatch
Command-specific help content definitions for the hench CLI — pure data/formatting, no command logic
Command validation with allowlist-based filtering and hazard detection — security enforcement logic
Guard-layer configuration interfaces and type contracts — pure type definitions, no logic
Path validation with defense-in-depth filesystem access control — security enforcement logic
Session-aware policy engine with cumulative rate limiting and resource budgets — stateful enforcement
Centralized gateway funneling all cross-package rex runtime imports through a single module
Concurrent execution metrics collection with ring buffers and aggregation — monitoring service
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for execShell from tools layer
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for process execution helpers from llm-client
Process lifecycle validation, orphan detection, and resource monitoring — stateful process management
Process concurrency limiter — shared infrastructure for controlling parallel execution
Monitors system memory usage — ongoing data collection and business logic
Throttling utility based on memory pressure — reusable control-flow helper
Process pool management — orchestrates lifecycle of worker processes
Tracks per-process memory — reusable instrumentation helper
Manages task execution queue — core business logic for sequencing work
Formatting helpers for queue output — pure presentation utility
Priority-based task scheduling — orchestration logic with state
Shared glob pattern helpers — reusable filesystem utility
Tool interface contracts — type definitions and constants for the tools layer
Routes tool calls to implementations — central tool dispatch logic
Shell command execution wrapper — low-level infrastructure utility
File operation tool implementations — reusable file I/O helpers
Git operation tool implementations — reusable git command helpers
Rex integration tool — API client/service layer bridging hench to rex
Shell tool implementation — reusable shell execution helper
Test runner tool — utility for invoking test commands from agent context
Task completion validation logic — reusable validation helper
API-based LLM provider implementation — API client and request orchestration
CLI-based LLM provider — service layer wrapping CLI subprocess calls
Codex-specific CLI provider variant — vendor-specific service implementation
Factory function for LLM clients — shared construction helper
Process execution utility — low-level subprocess helper
Help text formatting helpers — pure presentation utility
JSON parsing/handling utilities — reusable data-format helper
Core LLM client implementation — central service orchestrating model calls
LLM configuration loading and defaults — settings module
Shared LLM type definitions and interfaces — pure type companion file
Project-level configuration reader — settings module for project context
Pure constant exports and a companion type — no logic, just the canonical directory name definitions.
Defines the LLMProvider interface, ProviderInfo, ProviderCapability, StreamChunk and related types — no runtime logic.
ProviderRegistry class and createDefaultRegistry factory orchestrate vendor registration and config-driven provider selection — substantial business logic.
ProviderSession class manages active provider state, vendor switching, and config updates — stateful session management service.
Pure Levenshtein-based typo-correction helpers shared across all n-dx packages — no state, high fan-in expected.
Stateless parsing functions for API, CLI, and stream token usage formats — pure transformation helpers.
Manages the acknowledged-findings JSON store: disk I/O (load/save), hash computation, and pure store operations — a small persistence service.
Shared constants (model names, prompt templates), token-usage accumulators, format detection, and JSON repair utilities used across the entire analyze module.
Orchestrates the post-processing consolidation LLM call when proposals exceed the task ceiling — stateless pipeline with I/O.
Implements the LoE-driven recursive task decomposition pipeline: prompt building, LLM calls, response parsing, and tree replacement.
Multi-signal similarity scoring and union-find deduplication algorithm — pure functions used by both the analyze and recommend modules.
Pure formatting utility that produces a human-readable diff view between proposals and the existing PRD tree.
Requirements extraction engine: parses markdown/text/JSON/YAML documents into hierarchical proposals, optionally calling the LLM for disambiguation.
File validation utilities (extension checks, magic-byte detection, size limits, content warnings) used as a shared validation layer.
Interactive guided spec builder: manages the clarification Q&A loop with stdin, calls the LLM, and generates initial proposals from user input.
Manages module-level LLM client singleton and config, concentrates all @n-dx/llm-client cross-package imports, and provides the shared spawnClaude entry point.
LLM pipeline for modifying existing proposals from natural-language requests: prompt building, retries, validation, and quality checks.
Core proposal-building service: groups scan results into epic/feature/task hierarchies, deduplicates, and sorts by priority.
Main reasoning module: owns PRD prompts, project-context loading, proposal parsing and quality validation — also re-exports from analyze-shared and llm-bridge for backward compatibility.
Pure utility that compares scan results against the existing PRD tree using fuzzy matching to identify already-tracked items and update candidates.
LLM pipeline for generating PRD reshape proposals: prompt construction, LLM calls, Zod validation, and result accumulation.
Filesystem scanners (tests, docs, sourcevision output, package.json) that produce typed ScanResult arrays — domain service with I/O.
Pure validation and intent-classification functions for natural-language modification requests — no I/O, fully testable helpers.
CLI help content registry mapping command names to HelpDefinition objects — shared presentation utility used by CLI entry points.
MCP tool handler implementations: each function executes one tool's business logic against the PRD store and returns the MCP response.
Creates and configures the Rex MCP server — registers all tools with their Zod schemas and wires transport; acts as a server factory service.
Shared CLI input validation helpers (level, parent, status) that throw structured CLIErrors — pure guard utilities used across command handlers.
Pure conflict detection utilities using bigram similarity to identify duplicates between recommendations and existing PRD items.
Atomic PRD item creation service: validates, resolves parent relationships, checks DAG integrity, and persists items — all-or-nothing transaction logic.
Exports a single DEFAULT_WORKFLOW string constant — a configuration value defining the default agent workflow instructions.
Exports BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES constant catalog plus one small helper — primarily constant/data definitions with no I/O or orchestration
Pure functions only (no I/O, no side effects) for classifying work items by change significance — reusable helpers with no external dependencies
Orchestrates multi-step data collection: reads PRD from disk and git history, diffs completed items, builds enriched results — classic service layer with I/O and business logic
Combines pure filtering logic with git lifecycle detection (execFileSync, branch inspection) to produce filtered branch-scoped work items
Data access layer for persistent branch work records — validates with Zod, reads/writes JSON files, not a reactive state management store
Large analysis engine that generates architectural findings (god functions, tight coupling, dead exports, hub functions) from call graph data
Call graph computation and analysis service — builds the call graph data structure from source files
File classification engine that orchestrates algorithmic and LLM-based classification passes, manages incremental state, and merges results
LLM API client service — wraps @n-dx/llm-client with module-level state, lazy initialization, and a uniform callClaude interface
Reads PRD completion status from disk with rich output: per-item states, aggregate stats, timeline, and inconsistency detection
React component catalog analyzer — scans source files to detect and catalog UI components
Generates the CONTEXT.md output file — a pure transformation from analysis data to markdown text, high fan-in expected as a shared output generator
Batch enrichment orchestration — coordinates parallel LLM calls for zone enrichment in batch mode
Enrichment configuration constants (ZONES_PER_BATCH, PASS_CONFIGS, attempt configs) and prompt builders — configuration and parameters for the enrichment pipeline
Parses and validates LLM enrichment responses — reusable parsing helpers with no I/O
Per-zone enrichment orchestration — manages concurrent LLM calls for single-zone enrichment mode
Main enrichment orchestrator — drives multi-pass AI analysis across zones, coordinates batch and per-zone modes
Import graph analyzer — scans source files to build the dependency graph (edges, external imports, circular detection)
File inventory analyzer — walks the project tree and builds structured file metadata records
Generates llms.txt output — pure transformation from analysis data to LLM-readable text, shared output generator
Louvain community detection algorithm implementation — pure graph algorithm with no I/O, foundational utility for zone detection
Manifest file management — reads, writes, and updates the .sourcevision/manifest.json tracking analysis state
Derives prioritized next steps from zone findings — business logic that scores, groups, and sorts findings into actionable recommendations
Architectural risk assessment engine — computes risk scores, classifies zones into risk levels, emits deterministic findings
HTTP route detection service — scans source files using AST parsing and regex patterns to catalog server-side API routes
Pure token usage tracking helpers (create, accumulate, format) — small shared utilities with no I/O or domain logic
Workspace aggregation pipeline — orchestrates merging of multiple sub-analyses into unified output, writes all data files
Cross-repo crossing computation — resolves external npm imports to sibling workspace members and builds ZoneCrossing records
Sub-analysis detection and loading service — scans for nested .sourcevision directories and promotes their zones into the parent analysis
Single pure hashing utility (computeGlobalContentHash) extracted to break circular dependency — minimal shared helper
Analyzer module that processes and outputs zone data — business logic orchestration
Zone detection/analysis logic — core analysis pipeline step
CLI command handler for the MCP subcommand
CLI command handler for the serve subcommand
PDF generation/export service — data transformation and output orchestration
Markdown template generator — shared utility for producing PR markdown output
Canonical sorting utilities used across the pipeline
Server-side caching layer for aggregated data
Metrics tracking for concurrent executions — instrumentation service
Re-export gateway concentrating all sourcevision cross-package imports
Incremental task usage tracking — business logic service
Diagnostics service for PR markdown refresh operations
Re-export gateway concentrating all rex cross-package imports
Search index building and querying service
Server startup orchestration — initializes and wires server components
Task usage tracking and analytics service
Scheduled cleanup service for usage data
WebSocket connection management and event handling service
Shared data file constants and type definitions
Graph node culling utility shared between server and viewer
Request deduplication utility — shared infrastructure helper
Viewer bootstrap/entry point that initializes the client-side application
Graph physics simulation utility for the viewer
Graph rendering engine — orchestrates visualization logic
Data loading service for the viewer — fetches and prepares data for rendering
Rate limiting infrastructure for outbound calls
Gates DOM updates based on performance thresholds
Buffers and gates responses for performance
Batches DOM/state updates to reduce churn
Detects tab visibility changes to pause/resume polling
Gates tick dispatch based on tab visibility
Flow graph layout and rendering logic
CI helper script for PR validation checks
Script to refresh generated build artifacts
Web server orchestration entry point per CLAUDE.md
